title: Photographers
description: Photograph people, landscapes, merchandise, or other subjects. May use lighting equipment to enhance a subject's appearance. May use editing software to produce finished images and prints. Includes commercial and industrial photographers, scientific photographers, and photojournalists.

tasks.text: Adjust apertures, shutter speeds, and camera focus according to a combination of factors, such as lighting, field depth, subject motion, film type, and film speed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create artificial light, using flashes and reflectors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine desired images and picture composition, selecting and adjusting subjects, equipment, and lighting to achieve desired effects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Transfer photographs to computers for editing, archiving, and electronic transmission.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Use traditional or digital cameras, along with a variety of equipment, such as tripods, filters, and flash attachments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manipulate and enhance scanned or digital images to create desired effects, using computers and specialized software.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take pictures of individuals, families, and small groups, either in studio or on location.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enhance, retouch, and resize photographs and negatives, using airbrushing and other techniques.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test equipment prior to use to ensure that it is in good working order.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Estimate or measure light levels, distances, and numbers of exposures needed, using measuring devices and formulas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform general office duties, such as scheduling appointments, keeping books, and ordering supplies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review sets of photographs to select the best work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Set up, mount, or install photographic equipment and cameras.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine project goals, locations, and equipment needs by studying assignments and consulting with clients or advertising staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform maintenance tasks necessary to keep equipment working properly.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and assemble equipment and required background properties, according to subjects, materials, and conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct activities of workers setting up photographic equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Engage in research to develop new photographic procedures and materials.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mount, frame, laminate, or lacquer finished photographs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Send film to photofinishing laboratories for processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop visual aids and charts for use in lectures or to present evidence in court.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Load and unload film.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write photograph captions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up photographic exhibitions for the purpose of displaying and selling work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Produce computer-readable, digital images from film, using flatbed scanners and photofinishing laboratories.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Employ a variety of specialized photographic materials and techniques, including infrared and ultraviolet films, macro photography, photogrammetry and sensitometry.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: License the use of photographs through stock photo agencies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and print exposed film, using chemicals, touch-up tools, and developing and printing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
